I read the prompt and prior turns as external proposals. The prompt fixes the four-generator tame format: sigma,tau are distinguished, the tame relation tau^sigma = tau^2 is implicit, later generators lie in the pro-2 core, and exponents are supplied modulo M=85667662080.The prior file‚Äôs strongest surviving proposal is the Turn 9‚Äď12 relation; earlier candidates were not merely unproved but refuted by finite-tame or index-2 tests.

The current candidate remains:sigma,tau,x,y nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2 and ő∑=2ŌÄ on tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1.

The constants check as follows:ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑=‚ąí2ŌÄ ‚Č°50932630783(modM).I found one real correction to Turn 12. Its trivial-ŌĄ block conclusion is right, but the displayed quotient for P(T) is reversed in the prose. In the trivial tame-inertia block, with A=F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1), s=ŌÄmodf, and T the ŌÉ-shift, the linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

For even f, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís. The correct elimination is therefore

Y=P(T)X+YňČ,P(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,not the reciprocal. With this corrected P, the reduced X-block multiplier is still h(T)=1+P(T)+P(T‚ąí1).

For a nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1.

Thus h(ő∂)=0 would imply ő∂2s+1=1. Writing f=2af0 with f0 odd, the idempotent condition gives s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modf0), hence 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modf0). Therefore gcd(f,2s+1)=1, so no nontrivial f-th root is a zero of h, and h(1)=1. The trivial block is nonsingular exactly as Turns 10‚Äď12 claimed, but the corrected quotient matters for any written proof.

The new contribution this turn is an explicit finite-tame H1 -module identification. LetőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©,e¬†odd,¬†e‚ą£2f‚ąí1,and let Ue,f be the maximal pro-2 kernel induced by the candidate above this tame quotient. PutőĽ=i=0‚ąĎe‚ąí1ŌĄňČi‚ąąF2[őĒe,f].

The linearized relation is ‚Ąď=(ŌÉňČ‚ąís+őĽ)X+(1+őĽŌÉňČ)Y.Decomposing by the central idempotent őĽ, the nontrivial tame-inertia part is immediate:(1‚ąíőĽ)M‚ČÖ(1‚ąíőĽ)F2[őĒe,f],because on that part the Y-coefficient is 1. On the trivial tame-inertia part, A=F2[Cf], the presentation is

AX‚äēAY¬†/¬†((1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y).

If f is odd, s=0, so this is AX‚äēF2YňČ. If f is even, the corrected substitution above gives AX‚äēF2YňČ again. Adding the unramified pro-2 generator S=ŌÉf, which is őĒ-fixed, givesUe,f/ő¶(Ue,f)‚ČÖF2[őĒe,f]‚äēF2‚äēF2 as an F2[őĒe,f]-module.This is not just a rank check. It is the expected Kummer module shape for a tame Galois extension L/Q2: by the normal integral basis theorem for tame extensions, OL/2OL contributes the regular F2 [őĒ]-summand, while the two trivial summands correspond to the valuation class and the ‚ąí1 class. That gives a concrete equivariant strengthening over Turn 12, whose verification was mostly ‚Äúabstract DemuŇ°kin type plus orientation.‚ÄĚ

So the current status is:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1 I would still not mark this as a final proof. The surviving gap is now very specific: one needs a dyadic equivariant formation or rigidity lemma saying that a profinite group over the tame quotient is determined by these compatible data for every finite tame őĒe,f: the DemuŇ°kin kernel, the őĒ-module H1‚ČÖF2 [őĒ]‚äēF22, the cup product, the Bockstein/sign class, and the distinguished unramified extension class S=ŌÉf. Neukirch‚Äôs account of the odd-p Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg proof explicitly uses such DemuŇ°kin-formation uniqueness, and Schneider notes that the ramified dyadic case lacks the same ready-made method.Numdam+1 Jarden‚ÄďShusterman also record that Diekert‚Äôs dyadic result does not cover K=Q2.Mathematics at TAU

ESTABLISHED: The Turn 9‚Äď12 candidate survives another consistency pass. I corrected the trivial-ŌĄ block elimination to P=(1+T‚ąís)/(1+T), preserving nonsingularity, and proved the finite-tame Frattini quotient has the expected equivariant Kummer-module formUe,f/ő¶(Ue,f)‚ČÖF2[őĒe,f]‚äēF22.

GAP: The remaining missing proof is not another rank or cup-product computation; it is the equivariant dyadic formation/rigidity step. We still need a theorem showing that the compatible family of oriented DemuŇ°kin kernels with this őĒ-module, cup product, Bockstein class, and unramified extension class forces the full profinite group to be GQ2.

NEXT: Formulate and prove the needed rigidity lemma, probably by showing that the finite quotient action on U/ő¶(U), together with the DemuŇ°kin orientation and the distinguished S=ŌÉf, determines the extension 1‚ÜíUe,f‚ÜíEe,f‚ÜíőĒe,f‚Üí1 up to unique isomorphism compatible in (e,f).[BREAKTHROUGH]
